text: Schalke at 7th place feels SO wrong, but 9th place in the all-time table, recent fall into 2. Bundesliga and ZERO Bundesliga titles (though they came very close at times) sadly don't allow for more. Still, they are a giant with maybe the most loyal and die-hard fan base in all of German football.

Köln's Bundesliga title count is a bit deceiving. They were the kings of football and #1 on the all-time table during the early days of the Bundesliga in the 60s until Bayern dethroned them 17 years later. They have fallen off since, though not to quite the same extent as Kaiserslautern or Nürnberg.

Leverkusen's success is a more recent phenomenon. They are one of two clubs that are allowed to ignore the 50+1 rule, being directly owned (and financed) by Bayer AG, which many football fans in Germany view as an unfair advantage. They will most likely climb up this list as time passes by.
